source_pr.title: ddl: Fix issue with concurrent update getting reverted by BackfillData
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #58226, close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/58692

Problem Summary:
A concurrency test showed that when REORGANIZE PARTITION are copying non-clustered table rows in batches, if an update happens during such batch for the same rows included in the batch, then the batch will overwrite the updates with what the batch originally read.

### What changed and how does it work?
Reverted the use of table.AddRecord() for non-clustered tables and added the old row into the batch transaction, so the transaction would fail if the old row have been touched (if it already has been copied/double written and has not been touched, it will be skipped from being copied).

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
For non-clustered tables during REORGANIZE PARTITION data copying/backfill, if a row is updated at the same time the reorg is copying that row in a batch, it could overwrite with the state before the update.
```

symptoms: res := tkO.MustQuery(`select schema_state from information_schema.DDL_JOBS where table_name = 't' order by job_id desc limit 1`) (pkg/ddl/tests/partition/multi_domain_test.go)
failure_signature: res := tkO.MustQuery(`select schema_state from information_schema.DDL_JOBS where table_name = 't' order by job_id desc limit 1`) (pkg/ddl/tests/partition/multi_domain_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because DDL backfill (REORGANIZE PARTITION) could race with concurrent DML: for non-clustered tables, the backfill batch could overwrite a row that was updated concurrently (lost update / duplicate-row symptoms depending on timing). The fix avoids using AddRecord() for these backfill writes and instead involves the old row key in the same transaction (with assertions) so the backfill transaction will fail and retry if the row was touched concurrently, preventing the backfill from reverting updates.
fix_pattern: Reverted the use of table.AddRecord() for non-clustered tables and added the old row into the batch transaction, so the transaction would fail if the old row have been touched (if it already has been copied/double written and has not been touched, it will be skipped from being copied).

evidence: evidence: res := tkO.MustQuery(`select schema_state from information_schema.DDL_JOBS where table_name = 't' order by job_id desc limit 1`) (pkg/ddl/tests/partition/multi_domain_test.go)
